2026-04-17T17:42:28.7933677Z with:
2026-04-17T17:42:28.7935119Z   script: const { setupGlobals } = require('/home/runner/work/_temp/gh-aw/actions/setup_globals.cjs');
setupGlobals(core, github, context, exec, io);
const { main } = require('/home/runner/work/_temp/gh-aw/actions/compute_text.cjs');
await main();

2026-04-17T17:42:28.7936978Z   github-token: ***
2026-04-17T17:42:28.7937363Z   debug: false
2026-04-17T17:42:28.7937746Z   user-agent: actions/github-script
2026-04-17T17:42:28.7938243Z   result-encoding: json
2026-04-17T17:42:28.7938638Z   retries: 0
2026-04-17T17:42:28.7939380Z   retry-exempt-status-codes: 400,401,403,404,422
2026-04-17T17:42:28.7939908Z ##[endgroup]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2489905Z Repository permission level: write
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2824423Z text: AccountProperties::userReplicationPolicy and systemReplicationPolicy should tolerate missing fields
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2825616Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2825916Z ## Summary
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2826134Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2828022Z `AccountProperties::user_replication_policy` and `system_replication_policy` are currently required fields in the Rust SDK deserialization. When the service response omits these fields (e.g. the vnext emulator does not send `userReplicationPolicy`), deserialization fails and the SDK cannot connect.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2832514Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2832799Z ## Cross-SDK Behavior
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2833104Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2833515Z Both the .NET and Java SDKs treat this field as optional with a default fallback:
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2836116Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2837075Z - **.NET** (`DatabaseAccount.cs`): If `userReplicationPolicy` is missing from JSON, falls back to `new ReplicationPolicy()` (defaults: `minReplicaSetSize=3`, `maxReplicaSetSize=4`).
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2841227Z - **Java** (`DatabaseAccount.java`): Same pattern — `getReplicationPolicy()` checks for null and creates a default `new ReplicationPolicy()` with identical defaults.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2843374Z - **Service** (`DatabaseAccount.OnSave()`): The serialization path conditionally writes the field only if non-null, confirming it is not unconditionally required in the wire format.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2845764Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2845974Z ## Current Rust Behavior
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2846256Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2847399Z In [`account_metadata_cache.rs` (line 134)](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-rust/blob/release/azure_data_cosmos-previews/sdk/cosmos/azure_data_cosmos_driver/src/driver/cache/account_metadata_cache.rs#L134):
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2919574Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2920213Z ```rust
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2921228Z pub user_replication_policy: ReplicationPolicy,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2921880Z pub system_replication_policy: ReplicationPolicy,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2922428Z ```
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2922605Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2923364Z No `#[serde(default)]` or `Option<>` — a missing field causes a hard serde deserialization error.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2925278Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2925493Z ## Proposed Fix
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2925751Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2927582Z Add `#[serde(default)]` and implement `Default` for `ReplicationPolicy` with the standard defaults (`min=3`, `max=4`), matching Java and .NET behavior:
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2933910Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2934185Z ```rust
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2934663Z #[derive(Clone, Debug, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2935257Z #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2935770Z pub(crate) struct ReplicationPolicy {
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2936407Z     #[serde(default = "ReplicationPolicy::default_min")]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2937030Z     pub min_replica_set_size: i32,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.2937731Z     #[serde(rename = "maxReplicasetSize", default = "ReplicationPolicy::default_max")]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3002962Z     pub max_replica_set_size: i32,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3003666Z }
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3003861Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3004052Z // And on the AccountProperties fields:
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3005371Z #[serde(default)]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3005933Z pub user_replication_policy: ReplicationPolicy,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3006524Z #[serde(default)]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3007001Z pub system_replication_policy: ReplicationPolicy,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3007848Z ```
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3008053Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3008246Z ## Scenarios Where Field May Be Missing
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3008593Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3009843Z 1. **vnext emulator** — does not send `userReplicationPolicy` (current repro)
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3011013Z 2. **Service serialization** — `OnSave()` only writes the field if the in-memory object is non-null
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3012399Z 3. **Future service variants** — the wire format does not guarantee the field; all other SDKs are defensive here
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3014032Z title: AccountProperties::userReplicationPolicy and systemReplicationPolicy should tolerate missing fields
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3015343Z body: ## Summary
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3019906Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3023253Z `AccountProperties::user_replication_policy` and `system_replication_policy` are currently required fields in the Rust SDK deserialization. When the service response omits these fields (e.g. the vnext emulator does not send `userReplicationPolicy`), deserialization fails and the SDK cannot connect.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3025213Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3025390Z ## Cross-SDK Behavior
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3025637Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3026016Z Both the .NET and Java SDKs treat this field as optional with a default fallback:
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3026549Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3029097Z - **.NET** (`DatabaseAccount.cs`): If `userReplicationPolicy` is missing from JSON, falls back to `new ReplicationPolicy()` (defaults: `minReplicaSetSize=3`, `maxReplicaSetSize=4`).
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3031776Z - **Java** (`DatabaseAccount.java`): Same pattern — `getReplicationPolicy()` checks for null and creates a default `new ReplicationPolicy()` with identical defaults.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3035113Z - **Service** (`DatabaseAccount.OnSave()`): The serialization path conditionally writes the field only if non-null, confirming it is not unconditionally required in the wire format.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3036513Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3036698Z ## Current Rust Behavior
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3036966Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3038375Z In [`account_metadata_cache.rs` (line 134)](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-rust/blob/release/azure_data_cosmos-previews/sdk/cosmos/azure_data_cosmos_driver/src/driver/cache/account_metadata_cache.rs#L134):
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3173087Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3173407Z ```rust
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3173944Z pub user_replication_policy: ReplicationPolicy,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3174714Z pub system_replication_policy: ReplicationPolicy,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3175294Z ```
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3175484Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3176306Z No `#[serde(default)]` or `Option<>` — a missing field causes a hard serde deserialization error.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3176981Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3177266Z ## Proposed Fix
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3177506Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3178369Z Add `#[serde(default)]` and implement `Default` for `ReplicationPolicy` with the standard defaults (`min=3`, `max=4`), matching Java and .NET behavior:
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3179627Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3179790Z ```rust
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3180226Z #[derive(Clone, Debug, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3180828Z #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3181340Z pub(crate) struct ReplicationPolicy {
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3182006Z     #[serde(default = "ReplicationPolicy::default_min")]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3182599Z     pub min_replica_set_size: i32,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3183352Z     #[serde(rename = "maxReplicasetSize", default = "ReplicationPolicy::default_max")]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3184143Z     pub max_replica_set_size: i32,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3184600Z }
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3184788Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3184983Z // And on the AccountProperties fields:
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3185487Z #[serde(default)]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3190060Z pub user_replication_policy: ReplicationPolicy,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3190689Z #[serde(default)]
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3191152Z pub system_replication_policy: ReplicationPolicy,
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3191798Z ```
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3191980Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3192167Z ## Scenarios Where Field May Be Missing
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3192487Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3193053Z 1. **vnext emulator** — does not send `userReplicationPolicy` (current repro)
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3194110Z 2. **Service serialization** — `OnSave()` only writes the field if the in-memory object is non-null
2026-04-17T17:42:29.3195326Z 3. **Future service variants** — the wire format does not guarantee the field; all other SDKs are defensive here

2026-04-17T17:42:29.8350580Z # Agentic Triage
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8350789Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8351226Z You are a triage assistant for GitHub issues. Analyze issue #4211 and perform initial triage.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8351927Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8352118Z 1. Retrieve issue content using `get_issue`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8352452Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8352928Z    - If the issue is spam, bot-generated, or not actionable, add a one-sentence analysis comment and exit
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8354332Z    - If the issue is already assigned, has labels, or has a parent issue, exit
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8354838Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8355049Z 2. Use GitHub tools to gather additional context
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8355424Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8355869Z    - Do not run shell commands like `gh label list` - rely on labels inferred from repo context
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8356645Z    - Fetch comments using `get_issue_comments`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8357178Z    - Find similar issues using `search_issues`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8357766Z    - Find linked pull requests using `search_pull_requests`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8358357Z    - List open issues using `list_issues`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8358682Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8359040Z 3. Analyze issue content
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8359298Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8359461Z    - Title and description
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8359959Z    - Type: bug report, feature request, question, etc.
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8360506Z    - Technical areas mentioned
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8360943Z    - Severity or priority indicators
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8361399Z    - User impact
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8361828Z    - Crate names beginning with `azure` or `typespec`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8362390Z    - Changed files in linked pull requests
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8362729Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8363289Z 4. Write notes, ideas, nudges, resource links, debugging strategies, and reproduction steps relevant to the issue
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8363997Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8364244Z 5. Select appropriate labels from available repo labels
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8364647Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8365035Z    - All issues should have a #ffeb77 colored type label
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8365706Z      - `Client` - crates not starting with `azure_resourcemanager_`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8366580Z      - `Mgmt` - crates starting with `azure_resourcemanager_` or mentions of ARM or Resource Manager
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8367492Z      - `Service` - REST API or service behavior outside client SDK control
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8368422Z    - Tag issues from users without repo write access as `customer-reported` and `needs-team-attention`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8369548Z    - Tag questions (not bug reports or feature requests) with `question`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8370524Z    - Add `EngSys` service label for issues with scripts, workflows, or pipelines under /eng but not /eng/common
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8371501Z    - Use labels from similar issues for #e99695 colored service labels
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8372693Z    - If pull requests are linked to similar issues, check those pull requests' file paths against matching patterns in /.github/CODEOWNERS
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8374609Z      - If matches are found, use the `PRLabel` value in a comment above those lines (e.g. `PRLabel: %KeyVault`) to find related `ServiceLabel`s (e.g. `ServiceLabel: %KeyVault`) grouped with `AzureSDKOwners` and `ServiceOwners`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8376133Z      - Strip leading `@` from users and groups when assigning issues
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8376744Z      - Strip leading `%` from labels
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8377298Z      - Add #e99695 colored service labels from `ServiceLabel`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8378377Z      - If `Client` is applicable and there are `AzureSDKOwners`, assign to a random owner; if only `ServiceOwners` exist, add `Service Attention`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8379757Z      - Comment using this template when routing:
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8380127Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8380269Z        ```markdown
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8381144Z        Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team members best able to assist. cc {{ `AzureSDKOwners` each prefaced with `@` }}
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8383174Z        ```
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8383414Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8383818Z      - If `Service` is applicable, add applicable labels and `needs-triage`, then exit
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8384772Z    - All issues should have a #e99695 colored service label describing the relevant service
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8385892Z    - If unable to apply exactly one #ffeb77 type label and at least one #e99695 service label, apply only `needs-triage`
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8387111Z    - Add `needs-team-triage` if labels are added but `Service Attention` is not used and no person is assigned
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8387791Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8387951Z 6. Apply selected labels
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8388225Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8388402Z    - Use `update_issue` to apply labels
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8389123Z    - Do not apply labels if none clearly apply
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8390179Z    - Do not add comments beyond the markdown templates above
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8391208Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8391429Z 7. Add an issue comment with your analysis
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8391781Z 
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8392111Z    - Start with "🎯 Agentic Issue Triage"
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8392598Z    - Brief summary of the issue
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8393127Z    - Relevant details to help the team understand the issue
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8401618Z    - Debugging strategies or reproduction steps if applicable
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8402577Z    - Helpful resources or links related to the issue or affected codebase area
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8403523Z    - Nudges or ideas for addressing the issue
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8404134Z    - Break down into sub-tasks with a checklist if appropriate
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8405111Z    - Use collapsed-by-default GitHub markdown sections; collapse all sections except the short main summary
2026-04-17T17:42:29.8405802Z
